questions.question: What happens to a committee's key shares when the committee dissolves?
options: They are stored in a secure vault for future use
They are transferred to the next committee
They are destroyed and the key can never be reconstructed
They are archived on-chain for transparency
questions.correctAnswer: They are destroyed and the key can never be reconstructed

questions.question: In onlyswaps, solvers must be pre-approved and trusted by the dcipher committee.
How does onlyswaps differ from traditional lock-and-mint bridges?
options: onlyswaps creates wrapped tokens on the destination chain
onlyswaps uses native tokens and solvers front capital, verified by threshold signatures
onlyswaps relies on a centralized liquidity pool
questions.correctAnswer: onlyswaps uses native tokens and solvers front capital, verified by threshold signatures

questions.question: The core security assumption in dcipher is that at least a ______ of nodes in every committee behave honestly.
What prevents Sybil attacks in the dcipher network?
options: Staking requirements—each node requires locked capital
questions.correctAnswer: Staking requirements—each node requires locked capital

questions.question: Committee rotation helps prevent attackers from having time to corrupt a static set of nodes.
What is the main advantage of intent-based protocols like onlyswaps?
options: They allow users to specify desired outcomes while solvers compete to fulfill them efficiently
They process transactions faster than any other method
They don't require any smart contracts
questions.correctAnswer: They allow users to specify desired outcomes while solvers compete to fulfill them efficiently
